 Blackface stunt in Hawaii court angers judge 
 A Hawaii man convicted in a road rage attack wears blackface to his sentencing hearing, saying he's "being treated like a black man" in court. KHNL's Lynn Kawano reports.July 3, 2019

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Man who wore blackface to court jailed for life | The Independent | The Independent
[METADATA/INTRO]: Man who wore blackface to court jailed for life ‘You’re a menace to the public,’ judge tells Mark Char...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Man who wore blackface to court jailed for life 
 ‘You’re a menace to the public,’ judge tells Mark Char 
 - CommentsGo to comments 
 A man who painted his face black with a marker for his sentencing hearing has been ordered to serve life in prison. 
 Mark Char was told he would serve a mandatory life prison term with possible parole for attempted murder. 
 Honolulu Circuit Court Judge Todd Eddins also sentenced the 60-year-old to five years in prison for second-degree assault and one year for third-degree assault. 
 Attorney Keith Shigetomi, who withdrew as Char’s defence lawyer after the hearing, said it was Char’s choice to appear with his face and head blackened.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Hawaii man wears blackface, goes on strange rant before sentenced to life in prison for road rage attack | Fox News
[METADATA/INTRO]: A sentencing hearing for a man in Hawaii convicted of stabbing three people in a road rage attack took a shocking turn Monday when the man showed up to court in blackface and went on a bizarre rant that drew...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: A sentencing hearing for a man in Hawaii convicted of stabbing three people in a road rage attack took a shocking turn Monday when the man showed up to court in blackface and went on a bizarre rant that drew scorn from the judge. 
 Mark Char, 60, was sentenced to life in prison with the possibility of parole for second-degree attempted murder, plus six years in prison on convictions of second- and third-degree assault stemming from a 2016 attack on the H-1 freeway. 
 But when Char appeared in a Honolulu courtroom Monday, the 60-year-old stunned his attorney and the judge when he arrived with his face and head blackened. He then went on a rant during which he blamed the victims and explained his decision to wear blackface as playing "my part in your kangaroo court."
